name: ASAP4e
description: Speed-optimized ASAP4 variant with fast kinetics and a large fluorescence increase per action potential.

spectrum.name: ASAP4e
spectrum.note: One-photon excitation/emission spectra of ASAP3, ASAP4b and ASAP4e were measured in HEK293-Kir2.1 cells (Ext. Fig. 3e). ASAP4e and ASAP4b share an identical GFP chromophore environment, so the cpGFP excitation (~489 nm) and emission lineshapes are essentially identical; the digitized 1-nm-resolution custom curve (shared with ASAP4b) peaks at 489 nm (ex) and 511 nm (em). ASAP4 variants show slightly redder emission than ASAP3. The follow-up FORCE1s preprint (doi:10.64898/2026.04.07.717088, Fig. S1.2A) reports a measured ASAP4e 1P emission peak of 518 ± 4 nm in HEK293A cells (vs 511 nm for EGFP); peakEm here is set to the digitized custom-curve peak (511 nm) for consistency with the displayed curve and with sibling ASAP4b.

voltage.name: ASAP4e
voltage.note: Re-digitized from the F-V plot in Fig. 1B (ASAP4e = lavender, ASAP4.2 F413M) at the measured command-voltage steps, normalized to ΔF/F=0 at -70 mV with +30 mV anchored to the Table 1 100-mV value (+210%). Spans the full plotted range (-120 to +90 mV, reaching ~+435%); ASAP4e diverges above ASAP4b past ~+15 mV and keeps rising steeply. The prior curve's negative/mid-range points were over-steep.

kinetics.note: Amplitude-weighted mean of bi-exponential fit (Supplementary Table 1, ASAP4e at 22 °C, HEK293A voltage clamp). On (depolarization −70→+30 mV): τfast 3.93 ms (14.0%), τslow 20.64 ms → weighted mean 18.29 ms. Off (repolarization +30→−70 mV): τfast 8.40 ms (20.6%), τslow 17.47 ms → weighted mean 15.60 ms. Note: Table S1 is not column-swapped — it explicitly labels ASAP4b (τfast 2.62 ms) and ASAP4e (τfast 3.93 ms) at 22 °C; the main text instead pairs the 2.6-ms fast component with the larger 19% amplitude (= ASAP4b) and 3.9 ms with 14% (= ASAP4e). The amplitude→variant mapping is consistent between text and table, so the table values are used here.

Amplitude-weighted mean of bi-exponential fit (Supplementary Table 1, ASAP4e at 37 °C). On (depolarization): τfast 1.49 ms (14.5%), τslow 5.07 ms → weighted mean 4.55 ms. Off (repolarization): τfast 3.66 ms (68.6%), τslow 7.01 ms → weighted mean 4.71 ms. As expected, speed-optimized ASAP4e is faster than brightness-optimized ASAP4b (5.56/7.00 ms at 37 °C).

ASAP4e measured under 1P widefield voltage clamp at 33 °C in the FORCE1s preprint (Table S1.4). On (−70→+30 mV) is the amplitude-weighted mean of a bi-exponential fit: τfast 2.6 ms (34%), τslow 11 ms (66%) → 8.14 ms. Off (+30→−70 mV) was best fit by a monoexponential, τ 6.7 ms.

dynamicRangeData.note: ΔF100/F0 across the −70 → +30 mV (100-mV) step, voltage clamp in HEK293A cells, 1P. The full F-V sweep reaches 468% over all voltages (main text, Fig. 1b).

ΔF/F at +30 mV relative to −70 mV (100-mV step) read from the gray ASAP4e steady-state F-V curve in Fig. 1E, measured under 2P excitation at 33 °C in HEK293A cells (the curve reaches ≈3.0–3.3 ΔF/F0 at +30 mV). Larger than the 1P value (210%), consistent with the origin paper's finding that 2P ΔF/F is ≥35% higher than 1P for these sensors due to background suppression. The paper's quantified panel F reports the −70 → 0 mV (70-mV) response as 148 ± 29% (not a full 100-mV step), so 148% is NOT used as the dynamic range here.

sensitivityData.note: ΔFAP/F0 to a 3.5-ms FWHM, 100-mV AP waveform by voltage patch-clamp in HEK293A cells, room temperature, 1P (Table 1 reference value).
ΔFAP/F−70 to a 2-ms FWHM AP, HEK293A voltage clamp, 1P (order ASAP4b 19% < ASAP3 22% < ASAP4e 23%).
Naturalistic mouse-hippocampal single-AP waveform (1-ms FWHM) at 37°C, HEK293A voltage clamp, 1P.
3.5-ms FWHM AP waveform, 100 mV, room temperature, HEK293A cells, 1P.
Single AP in primary rat cortical neurons (ASAP4e-Kv, Kv2.1-tagged), whole-cell patch (Fig. 1h), one-photon. Distinct from the Table S1 HEK293A simulated-AP value.

apWidthData.sample: Mouse barrel cortex L2/3 pyramidal neurons, in vivo, whisker stimulation (ASAP4e-Kv)
apWidthData.note: Cross-measured in the ASAP6c paper (Lee et al. 2026, whose primary subject is ASAP6c). Optical FWHM (ms) of the single optical action-potential waveform under two-photon excitation (960 nm, 40 mW post-objective) at 400 fps in awake mouse barrel cortex during whisker stimulation. Mean of N=42 neurons read from the 'Optical FWHM (ms)' dot plot in Fig. 3d (ASAP4e-Kv mean marker ≈5.8 ms; cf. ASAP6b-Kv ≈5.8, ASAP6c-Kv ≈5.7; x-axis 5-8 ms). The mean line partly overlaps the gray data dots so ~5.8 ms is approximate. Temperature is in-vivo physiological (~37°C; not separately stated for Fig. 3).

apWidthData.sample: HEK293A cells, whole-cell voltage clamp, in vitro (2-ms FWHM command AP waveform)
apWidthData.note: Cross-GEVI value measured in the FORCE1s paper (McDonald et al. 2026, primary subject FORCE1s): in-vitro optical response width — FWHM of ASAP4e's fluorescence response to an isolated 2-ms-FWHM, 100-mV command spike waveform under whole-cell voltage clamp + 2P imaging at 33°C, HEK293A (n = 9). Imaging by resonant-scanning at 721 Hz. Digitized from the Fig. 1K dot plot (y-axis 2-6 ms, 95% CI bars): ASAP4e mean ≈ 4.2 ms (±~0.1 ms), intermediate between FORCE1s (≈4.9) and JEDI-2P (≈3.6). This is the response to a synthetic 2-ms command input (broadened by command width + kinetics), distinct from the in-vivo real-spike FWHM (≈5.8 ms at 400 Hz, ASAP6c paper, above). No exact number printed; digitized.

photostabilityData.note: ASAP4e-Kv showed 0 ± 10% fluorescence loss (→ ≈100% remaining) after 60 s of continuous 100-mW/mm² one-photon illumination in hippocampal SST+ interneurons in vivo (Fig. 2b). The near-zero net bleaching is genuine: some ASAP4e-Kv cells exhibit photoactivation that offsets photobleaching. Powers used were 100–250 mW/mm², normalized to 100 mW/mm² assuming linearity.

ASAP4e reference, in vitro (HEK293A) under 2P resonant scanning at 940 nm, 100 mW at the sample plane over a 40 × 635 µm FOV for 6 min (FORCE1s paper, Fig. S1.2B). ASAP4e (gray) normalized-2P-fluorescence curve reaches ≈ 0.60 at the 360-s (6-min) endpoint — slightly more photostable than FORCE1s (≈0.57) and much more than JEDI-2P (≈0.32). Sample-plane POWER (not an irradiance), so this 2P entry is display-only and does not normalize to the mW/mm² standard.

photobleach.note: Model-free photostability: t75% = time for fluorescence to fall to 75% of initial. Digitized from the ASAP4e curve in Fig. S1.2B (2P resonance-scanning, 940 nm, 100 mW at the sample plane over a 40x635 um FOV, 6 min, HEK293A; normalized 2P fluorescence). 2P power-only measurement (not mW/mm2) so it stands alone and is NOT intensity-normalized or comparable to 1P GEVIs. Measured alongside FORCE1s/ASAP4e/JEDI-2P in the same panel (they cross-overlay).

In-vivo 1P photostability digitized from Fig. 2b (ASAP4e-Kv, purple), hippocampal SST+ interneurons, continuous 453-nm widefield at 100 mW/mm². ASAP4e is so photostable it retains ~0.96 of F0 at 75 s — it never falls to 75% in the window, so NO t75 is stored (consistent with photostabilityData ~100% at 1 min). Curve kept for cross-overlay (vs ASAP3/ASAP4b, same figure). Mono fit r²≈0.79 reflects the near-flat, noisy in-vivo trace.
